title: Modified CTAB Procedure for Extraction of Plant DNA from Bird Feces
author: Carina Motta

steps.title: Preparation of Materials Verify that all...
steps.instruction: Preparation of Materials Verify that all solutions are prepared and autoclaved material is available Turn on the dry bath to 65°C Prepare three identical sets of numbered tubes (2 sets of 2 mL tubes and 1 set of 1.5 mL tubes) Record in the lab notebook the correspondence between the tube numbers and sample IDs

steps.title: Preparation of Samples Weigh 50 to...
steps.instruction: Preparation of Samples Weigh 50 to 100 mg (~80 mg) of fecal material for each sample directly into the tube. Add 200 μL of the sample alcohol to the separated feces.

steps.title: Extraction with CIA I Let samples come to room temperature
steps.instruction: Extraction with CIA I Let samples come to room temperature. In a fume hood, perform the first extraction with an organic solvent by adding 600 μL of CIA (chloroform-isoamyl alcohol 24:1).

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes in a microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes in a microcentrifuge at maximum speed (13,000 rpm) for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the tubes from the...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the tubes from the centrifuge, avoiding disturbance of the interface between the two phases.

steps.title: Extraction with CIA II Repeat the...
steps.instruction: Extraction with CIA II Repeat the extraction with 600 μL of CIA (steps 3/4/5).

steps.title: Centrifuge the tubes in a microcentrifuge...
steps.instruction: Centrifuge the tubes in a microcentrifuge at maximum speed (13,000 rpm) for 10 minutes.

steps.title: Carefully remove the tubes from the...
steps.instruction: Carefully remove the tubes from the centrifuge, avoiding disturbance of the interface between the two phases.

steps.title: Precipitate DNA Add ½ volume of...
steps.instruction: Precipitate DNA Add ½ volume of 6M NaCl (~270 μL) and 1/10 volume (54 μL) of 3M sodium acetate. Then add 2/3 of the volume of the aqueous solution (~400 μL) of cold isopropanol (-20°C). Mix gently to precipitate the nucleic acids.

steps.title: Place the tubes in a -20°C freezer for 2 hours
steps.instruction: Place the tubes in a -20°C freezer for 2 hours.

steps.title: Wash DNA Centrifuge the tubes at...
steps.instruction: Wash DNA Centrifuge the tubes at 13,000 rpm in a microcentrifuge for 10 minutes to form a pellet. Don’t worry if you can't see the pellet—it’s likely there, but it may be small and hard to see.

steps.title: Carefully pour off as much supernatant...
steps.instruction: Carefully pour off as much supernatant as possible without losing the pellet.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 500 μL of 70% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 500 μL of 70% ethanol.

steps.title: Wash the pellet with 500 μL of 100% ethanol
steps.instruction: Wash the pellet with 500 μL of 100% ethanol.

steps.title: Store in freezer at -20°C
steps.instruction: Store in freezer at -20°C

steps.title: Add 800 μL of CTAB buffer...
steps.instruction: Add 800 μL of CTAB buffer and 20 μL of Proteinase K with 10 porcelain beads. Place in the Minibeater for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Incubate the tubes in a water...
steps.instruction: Incubate the tubes in a water bath at 60°C overnight (~12 hours)

steps.title: Vortex the tubes and then invert...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tubes and then invert them for 5 minutes (at least 20 times) or until a homogeneous emulsion forms.

steps.title: Pipette the upper (aqueous) phase into a new tube
steps.instruction: Pipette the upper (aqueous) phase into a new tube. To expedite this step, set the pipette to 180 μL and withdraw four fixed aliquots (~720 μL). Even if some volume is left behind, this helps avoid contamination with the lower organic phase.

steps.title: In a fume hood, perform the...
steps.instruction: In a fume hood, perform the second extraction with an organic solvent by adding 600 μL of CIA (chloroform-isoamyl alcohol 24:1).

steps.title: Vortex the tubes and then invert...
steps.instruction: Vortex the tubes and then invert them for 5 minutes (at least 20 times) or until a homogeneous emulsion forms.

steps.title: Pipette the upper (aqueous) phase into...
steps.instruction: Pipette the upper (aqueous) phase into a new tube To expedite this step, set the pipette to 180 μL and withdraw three fixed aliquots (~540 μL). Even if some volume is left behind, this helps avoid contamination with the lower organic phase

steps.title: Let the pellet sit immersed for 2 minutes
steps.instruction: Let the pellet sit immersed for 2 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at 13,000 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 2 minutes at 13,000 rpm.

steps.title: Let the pellet sit immersed for 5 minutes
steps.instruction: Let the pellet sit immersed for 5 minutes.

steps.title: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 13,000 rpm
steps.instruction: Centrifuge for 5 minutes at 13,000 rpm.

steps.title: Mix 49
steps.instruction: Mix 49.5 μL of TE with 0.5 μL of RNase, and multiply by the number of samples.

steps.title: Elute DNA Resuspend the pellet in...
steps.instruction: Elute DNA Resuspend the pellet in 50 μL of TE with RNase.

steps.title: Place the open tubes in the...
steps.instruction: Place the open tubes in the dry bath (65°C) for ~2 hours until the alcohol evaporates.
